title: gagea pygmaea subsp. saxatilis
intro: Gagea pygmaea subsp. saxatilis, also known as Alpine Gagea, is a rare and lesser-known subspecies of the Gagea pygmaea plant, which belongs to the lily family (Liliaceae). The Alpine Gagea is a small, delicate plant with white, star-shaped flowers and is native to the high-altitude mountain regions of Europe. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The Alpine Gagea can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are believed to promote respiratory health, reduce inflammation, and soothe coughs, thereby providing health benefits for individuals suffering from respiratory ailments.
